name: Pledge Checkout Panel
description: Focused campaign checkout or reservation flow for selected reward tier, supporter details, payment/waitlist mode, and confirmation.

pledge-flow.description: Three-step pledge checkout with reward review, supporter details, payment or reservation, and confirmation note.

slots.selected-reward: Selected tier summary with price, inventory, and delivery note.
slots.supporter-form: Name, email, shipping/beta access fields, consent, and referral code.
slots.payment-or-reserve: Payment form, preorder authorization, or waitlist reserve mode.
slots.order-summary: Total, estimated delivery/access, guarantees, and campaign caveat.

waitlist-reserve.description: Lightweight reservation form for free beta or waitlist campaigns with referral capture.

slots.supporter-form: Name, email, role, referral code, and consent.
slots.confirm-action: Reserve spot or join waitlist button.

visual_brief: A focused checkout surface that keeps the retro campaign tone without making forms noisy. Use a single console/manual panel, clear steps, strong form labels, visible trust and fulfillment notes, and one primary completion action. Do not hide campaign caveats or shipping/access notes.

responsive.mobile: Single-column checkout with sticky bottom primary action only if it does not obscure form fields.
responsive.tablet: Reward summary above form; order summary below.
responsive.desktop: Two-column flow with form on the left and sticky order summary console on the right.

layout_hints.trust: Fulfillment, refund, beta access, and payment timing must be clear before confirmation.
layout_hints.mode_support: Support both paid pledge/preorder and free waitlist reservation campaigns.
layout_hints.form_design: Inputs should be readable and calm even inside the retro theme.

micro.step-change: stepper advances with short snap transition
micro.submit: button press state then loading/progress state

transitions.validation: inline field errors appear without moving the whole panel abruptly

keyboard: All fields must have visible labels.
Errors should be announced and linked to fields.
Stepper navigation must not skip required fields silently.
accessibility.focus_management: On step change, move focus to the new step heading or first invalid field.
